issues.description: requestUnlock() in lockService.js:241 does NOT verify EMI payment status before unlocking. Any admin/dealer can unlock an overdue device without payment confirmation, defeating the entire purpose of the EMI locking system.
issues.fix: Add EMI payment verification in requestUnlock() — query emi_schedules/emi_payments to confirm the device has no overdue balance before generating and delivering the unlock command.

issues.description: PAUT consumeToken() in pautService.js:117-119 missing AND revoked = false clause. A revoked PAUT (unlock) token can still be consumed if consumeToken is called directly, bypassing the verifyToken check that exists in verifyAndConsumeToken flow.
issues.fix: Add AND revoked = false to the UPDATE query in pautService.js consumeToken(), matching the pattern already used in padtService.js:119.

issues.description: PAUT and PADT tokens use HS256 (symmetric JWT). PRD mandates HSM-based asymmetric signing. If the signing secret is compromised, any attacker can forge valid unlock/decouple tokens.
issues.fix: Switch PAUT/PADT to RS256 or ES256 with asymmetric key pairs. Use the existing KMS infrastructure (kmsSigningService.js) for token signing.

issues.description: No rate limiting on /command (admin) and /unlock (admin/dealer) endpoints in lockRoutes.js. A compromised admin account could generate unlimited commands or rapidly unlock devices.
issues.fix: Add express-rate-limit middleware to /command and /unlock routes with appropriate limits (e.g., 5/hour for commands, 10/hour for unlock).

issues.description: PAUT_SIGNING_SECRET and PADT_SIGNING_SECRET have dev fallback values (pautService.js:17, padtService.js:17). If NODE_ENV is misconfigured in production, hardcoded secrets allow total token forgery.
issues.fix: Remove dev fallback entirely. Throw unconditionally if signing secrets are not set, regardless of NODE_ENV. Never use hardcoded secrets.

issues.description: Nonce value is logged in plaintext at lockCommandService.js:57. Nonces are security-critical for replay prevention. Log access by an attacker could reveal valid nonces.
issues.fix: Redact or hash the nonce before logging (e.g., log only first 4 chars or a SHA256 hash).

issues.description: verifyCommand() in lockCommandService.js:65 does not consume the nonce. The separate verifyAndConsumeNonce() method exists but callers must remember to invoke it — fragile design that risks replay attacks if missed.
issues.fix: Integrate nonce consumption into verifyCommand() or add a combined verifyAndConsumeCommand() method to enforce atomicity.

issues.description: wipeOnFailureEnabled: false in buildFullLockPolicy() (lockDeliveryService.js:223) contradicts PRD's Factory Reset Protection requirement. If a device evades the lock policy, there is no fallback enforcement.
issues.fix: Set wipeOnFailureEnabled: true or implement an alternative escalation mechanism when AMAPI policy application fails repeatedly.

issues.description: requestUnlock in lockController.js:222 always returns HTTP 200 regardless of status (statusCode = result.status === 'APPROVED' ? 200 : 200). Clients cannot distinguish success from failure.
issues.fix: Set appropriate status codes: 200 for approved, 422 for rejected/pending, matching the pattern used in requestLock.

issues.description: AWS KMS client in kmsSigningService.js:171 uses deprecated aws-sdk v2 which has known issues and is EOL.
